channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Palestinian officials say an Israeli strike on a school-turned-shelter in northern Gaza killed 15
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — An Israeli strike on a school sheltering the displaced in northern Gaza on Thursday killed at least 15 people, including five children, according to Gaza's Health Ministry.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: South Korean court acquits former police chief over deadly crowd crush
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — A South Korean court found the former police chief of the country’s capital and two other officers not guilty over a botched response to a Halloween crowd crush that killed nearly 160 people in 2022.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Pakistani police fire tear gas at protesting students as anger spreads over alleged on-campus rape
item.description: LAHORE, Pakistan (AP) — Pakistani police fired tear gas and charged at student protesters who ransacked a college building Thursday, as anger spread over an alleged on-campus rape.
item.dc:creator: BABAR DOGAR, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: The EU thought it had a new migration plan. But many leaders say it may be too little, too late
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — European Union leaders used a summit Thursday to seek ways to make the bloc a more hostile destination for migrants and asylum seekers following a recent surge in support for the extreme right, which has fomented opposition to foreigners.
item.dc:creator: RAF CASERT and MIKE CORDER, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Ukrainian president Zelenskyy heads to EU and NATO to seek backing for his 'victory plan'
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said Thursday that support from his embattled nation's Western allies is key to his “ victory plan ” to end the country's devastating war with Russia as he laid out details of the plan to European Union leaders.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, MIKE CORDER and RAF CASERT, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Death of ex-One Direction member Liam Payne at 31 shocks fans around the world
item.description: Fans, music-industry figures and fellow musicians are paying tribute to former One Direction member Liam Payne.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Debre And Kaitlyn Huamani, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Bangladesh court issues arrest warrant for ex-Prime Minister Sheikh Hasina over deaths of protesters
item.description: A special court in Bangladesh has issued arrest warrants for former Prime Minister Sheikh Hasina and 45 others, including her close aides, on charges of crimes against humanity during a student-led uprising in July and August that forced her to flee the country.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: North Korea says its revised constitution defines South Korea as 'hostile state' for first time
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korea confirmed Thursday that its recently revised constitution defines South Korea as “a hostile state" for the first time, two days after it blew up front-line road and rail links that once connected the country with the South.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Zelenskyy outlines his 'victory plan' to Ukraine's lawmakers, including a call to join NATO
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — President Volodymyr Zelenskyy told lawmakers Wednesday that Ukraine’s Western partners are increasing pressure to negotiate with Russia, but he hinted such talks would be unfavorable to Kyiv as he unveiled what he called his “victory plan” for the war.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB and HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: NATO shows no sign of letting Ukraine join soon and wants more details about its 'victory plan'
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — NATO showed no sign Wednesday it would invite Ukraine to join anytime soon as allies sought more information from Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy about his “victory plan” for ending the war with Russia.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Zeta drug lord continued to control cartel from inside a Mexican prison, says US indictment
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — A U.S. indictment unsealed Wednesday in the District of Columbia claims that the leader of one of Mexico’s most violent gangs continued to run an offshoot group, the Northeast Cartel, from inside a Mexican prison.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden to travel to Germany Thursday, delays African leg of trip
item.description: In Berlin, Biden will convene a meeting with German Chancellor Olaf Scholz to discuss continued support for Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Tanya Noury, NewsNation

item.title: Ex-Biden administration staffers who resigned over Gaza launch PAC
item.description: Two former officials who resigned from the Biden administration in protest over U.S. support for Israel have announced the creation of a political action committee focused on supporting candidates based on policy positions toward the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Italian Senate passes law making it illegal to seek surrogacy abroad
item.description: Italy’s Senate approved legislation Wednesday criminalizing the seeking of surrogacy in other countries by Italian citizens. Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni advocated for the policy, which is seen as targeting same-sex couples.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: In the heartland of Mexico's Sinaloa cartel, the old ways have changed and violence rages
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Cellphone chats have become death sentences in the continuing, bloody factional war inside Mexico's Sinaloa drug cartel. Cartel gunmen stop youths on the street or in their cars and demand their phones.
item.dc:creator: MARK STEVENSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden reschedules Angola trip for early December
item.description: President Biden intends to travel to Angola in the first week of December, the White House said Wednesday, rescheduling a trip that was scrapped as Hurricane Milton approached Florida last week.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Israel decides on possible Iran targets: 'Precise and deadly'
item.description: Israel reportedly has decided on which possible targets it could strike in Iran in response to an Oct. 1 barrage of missiles that Tehran fired at Israel.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Leaders from China-Russia-backed international organization meet in Pakistan
item.description: ISLAMABAD (AP) — Leaders and top officials from an international group founded to counter Western alliances met in Pakistan's capital on Wednesday and called for enhanced cooperation in areas including security, trade, and health.
item.dc:creator: MUNIR AHMED, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Set sail on world’s largest cruise ship, which can accommodate over 7,000 guests per voyage
item.description: Icon of the Seas is the world's largest cruise ship. With extensive dining options, record-breaking pools and breathtaking live shows, there's something for everyone.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ocasio-Cortez fires back at Fetterman’s 'bleak dunk attempt' on Gaza
item.description: Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez (D-N.Y.) fired back at Sen. John Fetterman (D-Pa.) over his “bleak dunk attempt” on the ongoing war between Israel and Hamas in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: An Australian police sergeant likely to be charged over a Nazi salute
item.description: An Australian state police chief apologized to the Jewish community after a sergeant allegedly performed a Nazi salute.
item.dc:creator: Rod McGuirk, The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Hezbollah vows to expand attacks in Israel after deadly strike in Lebanon's Christian heartland
item.description: AITO, Lebanon (AP) — The day after a deadly Israeli airstrike in northern Lebanon – far from Hezbollah’s main area of influence – the militant group's acting leader said it would aim rockets into more areas of Israel.
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB and BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: All about National Archaeological Museum in Athens, one of the world’s most prominent museums of its kind
item.description: The National Archaeological Museum houses artifacts dating back thousands of years. The collections are full of sculptures, artworks and more for guests to explore.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hereditary nobles have sat in Britain's Parliament for centuries. Their time may be up
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Like his ancestors for centuries, the Earl of Devon serves in Parliament, helping to make the laws of the land. But not for much longer.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Deadly Israel shooting ruled a terror attack
item.description: A police officer was killed and four others were injured when a terrorist opened fire near a highway in Ashdod, Israel, on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: North Korea is preparing to destroy northern parts of inter-Korean roads, Seoul says
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea said Monday it has detected signs that North Korea is preparing to destroy the northern parts of inter-Korean roads no longer in use, as the rivals are embroiled in soaring tensions over North Korea’s claim that South Korea flew drones over its territory.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Biden-Harris admin accused of ‘too little, too late’ to save the people of war-torn, famine-stricken Sudan
item.description: Critics say the Biden administration is in danger of waiting too long to stop the killing and help the people of Sudan get humanitarian aid, as it makes a last-minute effort to make a difference.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Interpol launches campaign to help solve 46 cold cases of women whose bodies were found in Europe
item.description: Interpol launched an initiative last week to seek the public's help in solving 46 cold cases of women whose bodies were discovered in Europe between 1982 and 2021.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israeli forces kill 2 Lebanese soldiers and injure 2 UN peacekeepers in separate strikes
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — An Israeli airstrike killed two Lebanese soldiers and wounded three on Friday, Lebanon's military said, just hours after the Israeli military fired on the headquarters of U.N. peacekeepers in southern Lebanon, injuring two of them for the second day in a row.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Yemen’s Houthi rebels attack a Liberian-flagged ship in the Red Sea
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Yemen's Houthi rebels attacked a Liberian-flagged chemical tanker on Thursday in the Red Sea, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, NewsNation
